questions.q: Explain the general electronic configuration of the s-block elements. Also, explain the significance of the noble gas configuration of the outermost shell of s-block elements.
questions.answer: The general electronic configuration of s-block elements is ns^1 or ns^2. The significance of the noble gas configuration of the outermost shell is that it is similar to the electronic configuration of the noble gas in the previous period. This configuration is stable and leads to low ionization energies and electronegativities.
marking_scheme: Mention the general electronic configuration of s-block elements (1 mark)
Explain the significance of noble gas configuration (2 marks)
Provide examples to support the explanation (2 marks)

questions.q: Describe the general characteristics of the alkali metals. Also, explain the trend in the physical properties of the elements in Group 1.
questions.answer: The general characteristics of alkali metals are that they are highly reactive and have one valence electron. The trend in the physical properties of the elements in Group 1 is that they have low melting and boiling points, higher reactivity, and lower density. This is due to the increase in atomic size and the decrease in ionization energy.
marking_scheme: Mention the general characteristics of alkali metals (2 marks)
Explain the trend in physical properties (2 marks)
Provide examples to support the explanation (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the method of obtaining alkali metals (s-block elements) from their ores. Also, describe the process of refining the metals obtained.
questions.answer: Alkali metals can be obtained from their ores using electrolysis of their molten chlorides or hydroxides. The process involves the removal of impurities using various methods such as fractional crystallization and electrolysis. The metals obtained can be further refined using electrolysis to produce high-purity metals.
marking_scheme: Explain the method of obtaining alkali metals from their ores (2 marks)
Describe the process of refining the metals obtained (2 marks)
Provide examples to support the explanation (1 mark)

questions.q: Describe the reactions of the alkaline earth metals with water. Also, explain the trend in the reactivity of the elements in Group 2.
questions.answer: The alkaline earth metals react with water to produce hydrogen gas and the corresponding hydroxide. The trend in reactivity is that it increases down the group due to the decrease in ionization energy and the increase in the electropositive character of the elements.
marking_scheme: Describe the reactions of alkaline earth metals with water (2 marks)
Explain the trend in reactivity (2 marks)
Provide examples to support the explanation (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the method of obtaining alkaline earth metals from their ores. Also, describe the process of refining the metals obtained.
questions.answer: Alkaline earth metals can be obtained from their ores using electrolysis of their molten chlorides or hydroxides. The process involves the removal of impurities using various methods such as fractional crystallization and electrolysis. The metals obtained can be further refined using electrolysis to produce high-purity metals.
marking_scheme: Explain the method of obtaining alkaline earth metals from their ores (2 marks)
